commit.message:
fix: make template removal and updates admin-only (#1619)

* Security: Make template removal and updates admin-only

Changes:
- Make template removal operations admin-only
- Make template update operations admin-only
- Update issue templates to require ownership proof from requesters
- Add comprehensive admin procedures to README

Admin verification now required for all privileged operations, with
clear documentation for verifying NPM package ownership.

* Use repository variable instead of secret for ALLOWLIST_ADMINS

Changed ALLOWLIST_ADMINS from GitHub secret to repository variable
across all workflows as per GitHub best practices for non-sensitive
configuration values.

Changes:
- check-permissions-workflow.yml: Use vars.ALLOWLIST_ADMINS
- remove-template.yml: Remove secrets parameter
- update-template.yml: Remove secrets parameter
- approve-template.yml: Remove secrets parameter
- decline-template.yml: Remove secrets parameter

Repository variables are more appropriate for admin allowlists as they:
- Are visible in the repository settings
- Can be easily audited and updated
- Don't require secret management for non-sensitive data

* Remove unused inputs from check-permissions-workflow.yml (#1627)
* docs: add email redaction guidance to README Proof of Ownership section (#1625)
* Fix label-triggered removal runs incorrectly posting "Template Removal Failed" for non-admins (#1626)

commit.message:
fix: bulletproof QR sizing and responsive booking form layout

Root cause: payment-card used CSS Grid (auto 1fr) which failed to
constrain the QR div, causing full-width overflow on mobile.

Fixes:
- payment-card: grid -> flex-column (centered QR at top, info below)
- qr-frame: display:inline-block + width:fit-content to shrink-wrap img
- qr-frame img: explicit min/max-width/height so img is always 180px
  on desktop, 120px on tablet, 100px on small mobile
- modal max-width: 720px -> 780px for more desktop breathing room
- booking-grid collapses at 680px (was 640px) for better tablet handling
- Mobile <680px: payment-card -> flex-row (QR 120px left, info right)
- Mobile <480px: time inputs stack single column, QR shrinks to 100px

commit.message:
fix: secret leakage in Debug Logs via JSON.stringify (#59)

* 🛡️ Sentinel: [CRITICAL] Fix secret leakage in JSON.stringify logs

The `formatValue` function in `src/logging.ts` serialized objects using `JSON.stringify(value, null, 2)` without any redaction logic. Any nested objects containing sensitive fields (like `Authorization`, `password`, `token`, or keys ending in `_key`) would be exposed in plaintext in debug and failure logs. This commit fixes the vulnerability by using a custom replacer function in `JSON.stringify` to scrub sensitive fields before they hit the disk or console.

Co-authored-by: hongymagic <302730+hongymagic@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(logging): cover secret redaction diagnostics (#63)

commit.message:
fix(agents): fix YAML parse error in codex-interface-manager description

The description field contained \\n escape sequences and \\'
escape sequences within a YAML double-quoted string. In YAML,
\\n inside double quotes is interpreted as a literal newline, causing
lines like 'Context: User wants...' to appear at the start of new
lines where the parser treats them as new mapping keys, resulting in
'mapping values are not allowed here'.

- Removed \\n sequences so the description stays on one line (matching
  the format used by all other agent files)
- Fixed \\'ll -> I'll (single quotes don't need escaping in YAML
  double-quoted strings)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
